source_pr.title: ddl: increase `SchemaOutOfDateRetryTimes` for robust test
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
Fix #14973 

### What is changed and how it works?

This CI failed caused by DDL's concurrency too high when running unit tests.
To fix this problem, we could increase the `SchemaOutOfDateRetryTimes` for test in `ddl` package.

I test this PR with CI more than 10 times, no reproduction of this problem.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Code changes

 - None

Side effects

 - None

Related changes

- None

Release note

 - None

symptoms: PR body states CI failures due to DDL concurrency in unit tests; proposes increasing SchemaOutOfDateRetryTimes to make tests robust.
failure_signature: PR body states CI failures due to DDL concurrency in unit tests; proposes increasing SchemaOutOfDateRetryTimes to make tests robust.
root_cause_explanation: Under high DDL concurrency in unit tests, schema propagation can lag and trigger transient "schema out of date" errors. The PR makes the DDL tests more robust by increasing `SchemaOutOfDateRetryTimes` in the `ddl` test package, effectively giving schema sync more retry budget under CI timing jitter.
fix_pattern: Increase `domain.SchemaOutOfDateRetryTimes` in `ddl` tests to reduce transient schema-out-of-date failures under high concurrency.

evidence: evidence: PR body states CI failures due to DDL concurrency in unit tests; proposes increasing SchemaOutOfDateRetryTimes to make tests robust.
evidence: diff sets `domain.SchemaOutOfDateRetryTimes = int32(50)` in ddl test init (ddl/db_test.go).
